class.name: FmodSystem
class.brief_description: Wrapper class for the FMOD::System object, providing audio system management and control.
class.description: FmodSystem wraps the FMOD Core API's FMOD::System object and provides comprehensive audio system management capabilities. This class handles system initialization, audio device management, sound creation, playback control, and recording functionality. The FMOD System is the core of the FMOD audio engine. It manages the mixer, channels, sounds, DSP effects, and output devices. You can create multiple System instances for advanced use cases, or use the global singleton via [FmodServer] for typical applications.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes the connection to the output device and returns the system to an uninitialized state without releasing the object. The system can be reinitialized by calling [method init] again.

method.name: create_channel_group
method.description: Creates a new [FmodChannelGroup] with the specified name. Channel groups allow you to organize channels for collective control of volume, pitch, and effects.

method.name: create_dsp
method.description: Creates a custom DSP (Digital Signal Processor) effect with the specified name. The created DSP can be inserted into the signal chain to process audio in real-time.

method.name: create_dsp_by_type
method.description: Creates a DSP effect of the specified built-in type. Use [method get_dsp_info_by_type] to query available DSP types and their information.

method.name: create_sound_from_file
method.description: Creates a [FmodSound] from a file path. If the path starts with [code]res://[/code], it will use [method create_sound_from_res] internally. The mode parameter specifies creation options using [enum FmodMode] flags, such as looping behavior ([constant FMOD_MODE_LOOP_NORMAL]), 3D positioning ([constant FMOD_MODE_3D]), or streaming ([constant FMOD_MODE_CREATESTREAM]).

method.name: create_sound_from_memory
method.description: Creates a [FmodSound] from audio data loaded in memory. The data array must remain valid while the sound is in use. This is useful for loading sounds from encrypted files or network streams. The mode parameter specifies creation options using [enum FmodMode] flags.

method.name: create_sound_from_res
method.description: Creates a [FmodSound] from a Godot resource path (starting with [code]res://[/code]). The file is loaded through Godot's file system and then passed to FMOD. The mode parameter specifies creation options using [enum FmodMode] flags.

method.name: create_stream_from_file
method.description: Creates a streaming [FmodSound] from a file. Streams decode audio data on-the-fly, which is memory-efficient for large files like background music. The mode parameter specifies creation options using [enum FmodMode] flags.

method.name: create_system
method.description: Creates and initializes a new FmodSystem instance with the specified maximum number of channels and initialization flags. The max_channels parameter limits the total number of simultaneously playing channels (range: 0-4096). The flags parameter specifies initialization options using [enum FmodInitFlags] bitmask.

method.name: get_channel
method.description: Retrieves a [FmodChannel] handle by its ID. Channels are assigned IDs sequentially as they are allocated.

method.name: get_channels_playing
method.description: Returns a dictionary with information about currently playing channels. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]channels[/code]: Total number of channels currently playing (including virtual voices). - [code]real_channels[/code]: Number of channels that are actually audible (not virtual).

method.name: get_cpu_usage
method.description: Returns CPU usage statistics for different parts of the FMOD Core API. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]dsp[/code]: CPU time spent on DSP processing. - [code]stream[/code]: CPU time spent on stream decoding. - [code]geometry[/code]: CPU time spent on 3D geometry processing. - [code]update[/code]: CPU time spent on system updates. - [code]convolution1[/code]: CPU time spent on convolution reverb processing. - [code]convolution2[/code]: CPU time spent on additional convolution processing.

method.name: get_default_mix_matrix
method.description: Retrieves the default mixing matrix used to convert audio from one speaker mode to another. This matrix maps source channels to destination channels for upmixing or downmixing. The hop parameter specifies the stride between matrix rows.

method.name: get_driver
method.description: Returns the index of the currently selected output driver for the active output type.

method.name: get_driver_info
method.description: Returns identification information for a sound device specified by its index. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]name[/code]: The name of the device. - [code]guid[/code]: The unique identifier of the device. - [code]system_rate[/code]: The default sample rate of the device. - [code]speaker_mode[/code]: The default speaker mode ([enum FmodSpeakerMode]). - [code]speaker_mode_channels[/code]: Number of channels in the default speaker mode.

method.name: get_dsp_info_by_type
method.description: Retrieves information about a built-in DSP type. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]name[/code]: Name of the DSP. - [code]version[/code]: Plugin version number. - [code]plugin_sdk_version[/code]: FMOD Plugin SDK version. - [code]num_input_buffers[/code]: Number of input buffers. - [code]num_output_buffers[/code]: Number of output buffers. - [code]has_create[/code]: Whether the DSP has a create callback. - [code]has_release[/code]: Whether the DSP has a release callback. - [code]has_reset[/code]: Whether the DSP has a reset callback. - [code]has_process[/code]: Whether the DSP has a process callback. - [code]has_setposition[/code]: Whether the DSP has a setposition callback. - [code]has_read[/code]: Whether the DSP has a read callback. - [code]has_should_i_process[/code]: Whether the DSP has a shouldiprocess callback.

method.name: get_file_usage
method.description: Returns file I/O statistics. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]sample_bytes_read[/code]: Bytes read for sample data. - [code]stream_bytes_read[/code]: Bytes read for streaming data. - [code]other_bytes_read[/code]: Bytes read for other purposes.

method.name: get_master_channel_group
method.description: Returns the master channel group to which all sounds ultimately route. Use this to control global volume, add master effects, or monitor overall output.

method.name: get_master_sound_group
method.description: Returns the master sound group that all sounds belong to by default. The master sound group can be used to set global playback limits and control collective behavior for all sounds.

method.name: get_network_proxy
method.description: Returns the URL of the proxy server used for internet streaming connections.

method.name: get_network_timeout
method.description: Returns the timeout in milliseconds for network streams.

method.name: get_num_drivers
method.description: Returns the number of available output drivers for the currently selected output type.

method.name: get_output
method.description: Returns the currently selected output interface type used to run the mixer.

method.name: get_output_handle
method.description: Returns the output type-specific native handle. For Windows, this is typically a WASAPI or DirectSound device handle.

method.name: get_record_driver_info
method.description: Returns identification information for a recording device specified by its index. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]name[/code]: The name of the recording device. - [code]guid[/code]: The unique identifier of the device. - [code]system_rate[/code]: The default sample rate. - [code]speaker_mode[/code]: The speaker mode ([enum FmodSpeakerMode]). - [code]speaker_mode_channels[/code]: Number of channels. - [code]state[/code]: Driver state flags.

method.name: get_record_num_drivers
method.description: Returns information about available recording devices. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]num_drivers[/code]: Total number of recording devices available. - [code]num_connected[/code]: Number of currently connected recording devices.

method.name: get_record_position
method.description: Returns the current recording position in PCM samples for the specified recording device.

method.name: get_speaker_mode_channels
method.description: Returns the number of channels for the specified speaker mode.

method.name: get_version
method.description: Returns the FMOD version information. The returned dictionary contains: - [code]version[/code]: The FMOD version string in format "product.major.minor". - [code]build_number[/code]: The FMOD build number string.

method.name: init
method.description: Initializes the FMOD System with the specified maximum number of channels and initialization flags. The max_channels parameter limits the total number of simultaneously playing channels (range: 0-4096). The flags parameter is a bitmask of [enum FmodInitFlags] values.

method.name: is_recording
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the specified recording device is currently recording.

method.name: mixer_resume
method.description: Resumes the mixer thread and re-acquires access to the audio hardware. Use after [method mixer_suspend] to restore audio processing.

method.name: mixer_suspend
method.description: Suspends the mixer thread, relinquishing use of the audio hardware while maintaining internal state. Useful for handling audio focus changes on mobile platforms.

method.name: play_dsp
method.description: Plays a DSP and its input signals on a channel, routed through the specified channel group. If paused is [code]true[/code], the channel starts in a paused state and won't produce sound until [method FmodChannel.set_paused] is called with [code]false[/code].

method.name: play_sound
method.description: Plays a sound on a channel, routed through the specified channel group. If paused is [code]true[/code], the channel starts in a paused state and won't produce sound until [method FmodChannel.set_paused] is called with [code]false[/code]. Returns a [FmodChannel] handle that can be used to control playback.

method.name: record_start
method.description: Starts recording from the specified device into the provided sound object. If loop is [code]true[/code], recording will wrap around to the beginning of the sound buffer when it reaches the end.

method.name: record_stop
method.description: Stops recording from the specified device.

method.name: release
method.description: Closes the system connection and releases all associated resources. After calling this method, the system object is no longer valid and should not be used.

method.name: set_driver
method.description: Sets the output driver for the currently selected output type. Use [method get_driver_info] to enumerate available drivers.

method.name: set_network_proxy
method.description: Sets a proxy server URL for all subsequent internet streaming connections. Pass an empty string to disable proxy.

method.name: set_network_timeout
method.description: Sets the timeout in milliseconds for network streams. This affects how long FMOD waits for data before considering a stream stalled.

method.name: set_output
method.description: Sets the output interface type used to run the mixer. Must be called before [method init]. Common options include [constant FMOD_OUTPUT_TYPE_AUTODETECT], [constant FMOD_OUTPUT_TYPE_WASAPI] (Windows), and [constant FMOD_OUTPUT_TYPE_NOSOUND] (silent mode).

method.name: system_is_null
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the internal FMOD System object is null (not created).

method.name: system_is_valid
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the internal FMOD System object is valid and ready for use.

method.name: update
method.description: Updates the FMOD System. This should be called regularly (typically every frame) to process streams, 3D positioning, and non-realtime output modes. When using [constant FMOD_INIT_FLAG_STREAM_FROM_UPDATE] or [constant FMOD_INIT_FLAG_MIX_FROM_UPDATE], this method is even more critical as it drives the audio processing.

method.name: set_software_channels
method.description: Sets the maximum number of software channels for the mixer. This determines how many channels can be mixed in software when hardware voices are exhausted. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_software_channels
method.description: Returns the maximum number of software channels.

method.name: set_software_format
method.description: Sets the output format for the software mixer. - [param sample_rate]: Output sample rate in Hz (typically 44100 or 48000) - [param speaker_mode]: Speaker output configuration (stereo, 5.1, etc.) - [param num_raw_speakers]: Number of speakers for raw speaker mode Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_software_format
method.description: Returns the current software format settings as a Dictionary with: - [code]sample_rate[/code]: Current sample rate - [code]speaker_mode[/code]: Current speaker mode - [code]num_raw_speakers[/code]: Number of raw speakers

method.name: set_dsp_buffer_size
method.description: Sets the DSP buffer size for the software mixer. Smaller buffer sizes reduce latency but increase CPU usage and risk of audio dropouts. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_dsp_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the current DSP buffer size settings as a Dictionary with: - [code]buffer_length[/code]: Length of each buffer in samples - [code]num_buffers[/code]: Number of buffers in the ring buffer

method.name: set_stream_buffer_size
method.description: Sets the default buffer size for streaming sounds. This affects how much data is read ahead for streamed audio files.

method.name: get_stream_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the current stream buffer size settings as a Dictionary with: - [code]file_buffer_size[/code]: Size of the stream buffer - [code]file_buffer_size_type[/code]: The time unit used for the buffer size

method.name: set_speaker_position
method.description: Sets the position of a speaker in the speaker configuration. This allows custom speaker positioning for non-standard speaker setups. The position is specified in 2D coordinates relative to the listener.

method.name: get_speaker_position
method.description: Returns the position of the specified speaker as a Dictionary with: - [code]x[/code]: X coordinate - [code]y[/code]: Y coordinate - [code]active[/code]: Whether the speaker is active

method.name: set_doppler_scale
method.description: Sets the scaling factor for 3D doppler effect calculations. Higher values exaggerate the doppler effect, lower values reduce it. A value of 1.0 represents the default scaling.

method.name: get_doppler_scale
method.description: Returns the current doppler scale factor.

method.name: set_distance_factor
method.description: Sets the distance factor for 3D spatial audio. This converts the units used in 3D positioning (e.g., meters) to FMOD's internal units. Default is 1.0 (1 unit = 1 meter).

method.name: get_distance_factor
method.description: Returns the current distance factor for 3D audio.

method.name: set_rolloff_scale
method.description: Sets the scaling factor for 3D distance rolloff attenuation. Higher values cause sounds to attenuate more quickly with distance. Default is 1.0.

method.name: get_rolloff_scale
method.description: Returns the current rolloff scale factor.

method.name: set_3d_num_listeners
method.description: Sets the number of 3D listeners in the scene. Multiple listeners can be used for split-screen multiplayer or other scenarios. Maximum number of listeners is platform-dependent.

method.name: get_3d_num_listeners
method.description: Returns the number of active 3D listeners.

method.name: set_3d_rolloff_callback_enabled
method.description: Enables or disables the custom 3D rolloff callback. When enabled, the [_calculate_3d_rolloff] virtual method is called for custom distance attenuation calculations.

method.name: is_3d_rolloff_callback_enabled
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the custom 3D rolloff callback is enabled.

method.name: set_max_mpeg_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of MPEG (MP3) codecs to allocate. Each playing MPEG sound requires a codec. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_mpeg_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated MPEG codecs.

method.name: set_max_adpcm_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of IMA-ADPCM codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_adpcm_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated ADPCM codecs.

method.name: set_max_xma_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of XMA codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_xma_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated XMA codecs.

method.name: set_max_vorbis_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of Vorbis codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_vorbis_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated Vorbis codecs.

method.name: set_max_at9_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of ATRAC9 (AT9) codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_at9_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated AT9 codecs.

method.name: set_max_fadpcm_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of FADPCM codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_fadpcm_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated FADPCM codecs.

method.name: set_max_opus_codecs
method.description: Sets the maximum number of Opus codecs to allocate. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_opus_codecs
method.description: Returns the maximum number of allocated Opus codecs.

method.name: set_asio_num_channels
method.description: Sets the number of ASIO channels for input/output. Only applicable when using ASIO output mode.

method.name: get_asio_num_channels
method.description: Returns the number of configured ASIO channels.

method.name: set_vol0_virtual_vol
method.description: Sets the virtual voice threshold volume. Sounds with volume below this threshold become virtual voices.

method.name: get_vol0_virtual_vol
method.description: Returns the current virtual voice threshold volume.

method.name: set_default_decode_buffer_size
method.description: Sets the default decode buffer size for streams. Larger buffers reduce the risk of buffer underruns but use more memory.

method.name: get_default_decode_buffer_size
method.description: Returns the default decode buffer size for streams.

method.name: set_profile_port
method.description: Sets the TCP port for FMOD Profiler connections. Use with [constant FMOD_INIT_FLAG_PROFILE_ENABLE] to enable profiling.

method.name: get_profile_port
method.description: Returns the configured FMOD Profiler port.

method.name: set_geometry_max_fade_time
method.description: Sets the maximum fade time for geometry occlusion changes. When sound is occluded by geometry, the transition is smoothed over this duration (in milliseconds).

method.name: get_geometry_max_fade_time
method.description: Returns the maximum geometry occlusion fade time.

method.name: set_distance_filter_center_freq
method.description: Sets the center frequency for the distance filter. Used with [constant FMOD_INIT_FLAG_CHANNEL_DISTANCEFILTER].

method.name: get_distance_filter_center_freq
method.description: Returns the distance filter center frequency.

method.name: set_reverb_3d_instance
method.description: Sets the global reverb instance to use for 3D reverb calculations.

method.name: get_reverb_3d_instance
method.description: Returns the current 3D reverb instance index.

method.name: set_dsp_buffer_pool_size
method.description: Sets the DSP buffer pool size for intermediate mixing buffers. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_dsp_buffer_pool_size
method.description: Returns the DSP buffer pool size.

method.name: set_resampler_method
method.description: Sets the resampling method for pitch changes. Higher quality methods use more CPU. See [enum FmodResamplerMethod].

method.name: get_resampler_method
method.description: Returns the current resampler method.

method.name: set_random_seed
method.description: Sets the random seed for FMOD's internal random number generator. Affects randomized features like chorus and flange effects.

method.name: get_random_seed
method.description: Returns the current random seed.

method.name: set_max_convolution_threads
method.description: Sets the maximum number of CPU threads for convolution reverb processing.

method.name: get_max_convolution_threads
method.description: Returns the maximum number of convolution threads.

method.name: set_max_spatial_objects
method.description: Sets the maximum number of spatial audio objects for object-based audio output. Must be called before [method init].

method.name: get_max_spatial_objects
method.description: Returns the maximum number of spatial objects.

method.name: create_geometry
method.description: Creates a geometry object for 3D occlusion calculations. Geometry is used to simulate sound occlusion by physical objects in the scene.

method.name: create_reverb_3d
method.description: Creates a 3D reverb zone for spatial audio reverberation effects. Returns a [FmodReverb3D] object that can be positioned in 3D space and configured with reverb parameters. Use this to create environmental audio effects like caves, halls, or rooms.

method.name: create_sound_group
method.description: Creates a new [FmodSoundGroup] with the specified name. Sound groups allow you to organize sounds into categories with shared playback limits and collective control. Useful for managing weapon sounds, ambient sounds, or dialogue with max audible limits.

method.name: set_3d_max_world_size
method.description: Sets the maximum world size for 3D spatial audio calculations. This affects the precision of spatial calculations.

method.name: get_3d_max_world_size
method.description: Returns the maximum world size for 3D spatial audio.

method.name: load_geometry
method.description: Loads pre-saved geometry data from a PackedByteArray. Useful for loading complex geometry from disk.

method.name: get_geometry_occlusion
method.description: Calculates occlusion between a listener and a sound source position. Returns a Dictionary with occlusion values for direct and reverb paths.

method.name: lock_dsp
method.description: Locks the DSP engine mutex. Use this when making multiple DSP calls that need to be atomic. Always pair with [method unlock_dsp].

method.name: unlock_dsp
method.description: Unlocks the DSP engine mutex. Must be called after [method lock_dsp].

method.name: set_3d_listener_attributes
method.description: Sets the position, velocity, and orientation of a 3D listener. The listener index specifies which listener to configure (for multi-listener setups). - [param position]: World position of the listener - [param velocity]: Velocity for doppler calculations - [param forward]: Forward direction vector (must be normalized) - [param up]: Up direction vector (must be normalized)

method.name: get_3d_listener_attributes
method.description: Returns the 3D attributes of the specified listener as a Dictionary: - [code]position[/code]: World position (Vector3) - [code]velocity[/code]: Velocity (Vector3) - [code]forward[/code]: Forward direction (Vector3) - [code]up[/code]: Up direction (Vector3)

method.name: set_reverb_properties
method.description: Sets the reverb properties for the specified global reverb instance. Parameters control various aspects of the reverb effect: - [param decay_time]: Reverberation decay time in seconds - [param early_delay]: Initial reflection delay time - [param late_delay]: Late reverberation delay time - [param hf_reference]: High-frequency reference in Hz - [param hf_decay_ratio]: High-frequency decay ratio - [param diffusion]: Diffusion amount (echo density) - [param density]: Density of late reverberation - [param low_shelf_frequency]: Low shelf filter frequency - [param low_shelf_gain]: Low shelf filter gain - [param high_cut]: High cut frequency - [param early_late_mix]: Mix between early and late reflections - [param wet_level]: Wet (effected) signal level in dB

method.name: get_reverb_properties
method.description: Returns the reverb properties for the specified global reverb instance as a Dictionary with all parameters.

method.name: attach_channel_group_to_port
method.description: Attaches a channel group's output to an audio port on the output driver. This allows routing audio to specific output destinations like controller speakers or auxiliary outputs. See [enum FmodPortType] for available port types.

method.name: detach_channel_group_from_port
method.description: Detaches a channel group from its assigned audio port. The channel group will return to normal routing to the master output.

constants.constant: Normal initialization, no special flags.
No stream thread is created internally. Streams are driven from [method update]. Useful for non-realtime outputs.
No mixer thread is created internally. Mixing is driven from [method update]. Only applicable for polling-based output modes.
3D calculations use right-handed coordinates instead of the default left-handed coordinates.
Enables hard clipping of output values greater than 1.0 or less than -1.0.
Enables lowpass gain and 3D occlusion features on channels, and automatic use of the Geometry API.

All 3D-based voices have software lowpass and highpass filters added to the DSP chain as an automatic distance-based bandpass filter. Use [code]set_advanced_settings[/code] to adjust center frequencies.
Enables TCP/IP profiling host, allowing FMOD Studio or FMOD Profiler to connect and view memory, CPU, and DSP graphs in real-time.
Sounds with volume 0 become virtual voices and are not processed except for position updates.
When using the geometry engine, only process the closest polygon instead of accumulating all polygons intersecting the sound-to-listener line.
When using 5.1 speaker mode with stereo output devices, use Dolby Pro Logic II downmix algorithm instead of the default stereo downmix.
Disables thread safety for API calls. Only use if FMOD is called from a single thread and Studio API is not used!
Enables level metering on all DSPs. This is slower but provides level information for all units in the DSP graph.
Enables memory allocation tracking. Currently only useful when using the Studio API. Increases memory usage and reduces performance.
Automatically select the most appropriate output mode for the platform.
Unknown output type, returned when the system cannot determine the output mode.
No sound output. All calls succeed but no audio is produced. Useful for servers or offline processing.
Write output to a WAV file instead of playing through speakers.
Non-realtime no-sound mode. Calls to [method update] produce audio output without real-time constraints.
Non-realtime WAV writer mode. Writes audio to a WAV file without real-time constraints, allowing faster-than-realtime encoding.
Windows Audio Session API. Default output mode on Windows Vista and later.
Audio Stream Input/Output. Low-latency professional audio interface for Windows.
PulseAudio output. Default on modern Linux distributions.
Advanced Linux Sound Architecture. Legacy Linux audio output.
Core Audio. Default output mode on macOS and iOS.
Android AudioTrack output. Legacy Android audio output.
OpenSL ES output. Android native audio API.
PlayStation AudioOut output.
PlayStation Audio3D output.
Web Audio API output for HTML5/WebAssembly builds.
Nintendo Switch nn::audio output.
Windows Sonic output with spatial audio support.
Android AAudio output. Modern low-latency Android audio API.
Web Audio Worklet output for improved HTML5 audio performance.
Xbox Phase output.
OpenHarmony OHAudio output.
Maximum output type value, used for bounds checking.
Forces the enum to be 32-bit size.
Default speaker mode for the selected output device.
Raw speaker mode. Speaker mapping is disabled, channels are output as-is.
Mono output (1 channel).
Stereo output (2 channels: left, right).
Quadraphonic output (4 channels: front left, front right, surround left, surround right).
Surround output (5 channels: front left, front right, center, surround left, surround right).
5.1 surround output (6 channels: front left, front right, center, LFE, surround left, surround right).
7.1 surround output (8 channels: front left, front right, center, LFE, surround left, surround right, back left, back right).
7.1.4 surround output (12 channels: 7.1 plus 4 overhead channels for Dolby Atmos).
Maximum speaker mode value, used for bounds checking.
Forces the enum to be 32-bit size.
Default sound creation mode.
Sound does not loop. Playback stops at the end.
Sound loops normally, jumping from end back to start.
Sound loops bidirectionally, playing forward then backward (ping-pong).
2D sound that ignores 3D positioning and attenuation.
3D sound that is affected by 3D positioning and attenuation settings.
Creates a stream for the sound. Audio is decoded on-the-fly, saving memory for large files.
Loads the entire sound into memory as a sample. Enables fast seeking and repeated playback.
Creates a compressed sample that stays compressed in memory, decoding in real-time during playback. Saves memory but uses more CPU.
Opens a user-created sound with custom read callbacks.
Opens a sound from memory. The memory buffer is copied by FMOD.
Opens a sound from memory using the provided pointer directly without copying. The application must keep the memory valid during playback.
Opens a raw audio file with no format parsing. Requires manual specification of format parameters.
Opens the file for metadata reading only. No audio data is loaded and the sound cannot be played.
Calculates accurate length and position information for VBR formats like MP3. Increases opening time.
For MP2/MP3 files: searches for sync bytes to find the first frame. Increases opening time but improves compatibility with some files.
Opens the sound asynchronously without blocking. Check [method FmodSound.get_open_state] to determine when the sound is ready.
Creates a unique sound instance that is not shared with other sounds referencing the same file.
3D sound position is relative to the listener's head orientation.
3D sound position is in world coordinates (default for 3D sounds).
3D sound uses inverse distance rolloff model (volume decreases with 1/distance).
3D sound uses linear distance rolloff model.
3D sound uses linear squared distance rolloff model.
3D sound uses inverse tapered rolloff model with smooth transition at max distance.
3D sound uses a custom rolloff curve defined by [method FmodChannel.set_3d_custom_rolloff].
3D sound ignores geometry-based occlusion and environmental effects.
Ignores metadata tags in the sound file, speeding up loading.
Uses less memory when loading sounds, at the cost of some features and compatibility.
Virtual channels resume from the start of the sound when they become real, rather than from where they went virtual.
Time in milliseconds.
Time in PCM (Pulse Code Modulation) samples.
Time in bytes of PCM data.
Time in raw file bytes (compressed data).
Time as a fractional PCM sample position.
For module formats (MOD/XM/IT/S3M): pattern order position.
For module formats (MOD/XM/IT/S3M): row within the current pattern.
For module formats (MOD/XM/IT/S3M): pattern index.
No speaker (invalid or disabled).
Front left speaker.
Front right speaker.
Front center speaker (dialogue).
Low frequency effects/subwoofer (LFE).
Surround left speaker.
Surround right speaker.
Back left speaker (7.1).
Back right speaker (7.1).
Top front left speaker (Dolby Atmos).
Top front right speaker (Dolby Atmos).
Top back left speaker (Dolby Atmos).
Top back right speaker (Dolby Atmos).
Maximum speaker index.
Forces the enum to be 32-bit size.
Default resampler (platform-dependent, usually linear).
No interpolation (nearest neighbor, lowest quality).
Linear interpolation (fast, good quality).
Cubic interpolation (slower, better quality).
Spline interpolation (slowest, best quality).
Maximum resampler method value.
Forces the enum to be 32-bit size.
Background music port. Use [code]FMOD_PORT_INDEX_NONE[/code] as the port index.
Copyrighted background music port. Use [code]FMOD_PORT_INDEX_NONE[/code] as the port index.
Voice chat port. Use the platform-specific user ID as the port index.
Controller speaker port (e.g., DualShock 4 speaker, Nintendo Switch controller). Use the platform-specific user ID as the port index.
Personal audio device port (e.g., headphones, headset). Use the platform-specific user ID as the port index.
Controller vibration port. Use the platform-specific user ID as the port index.
Auxiliary output port. Use [code]FMOD_PORT_INDEX_NONE[/code] as the port index.
Passthrough output port. Use [code]FMOD_PORT_INDEX_NONE[/code] as the port index.
VR controller vibration port. Use the platform-specific user ID as the port index.
Maximum port type value.
